other_observers: many, see submissions; Mark Miller on 04/25/08
specific_location: Left Hand Creek, under bridge over that creek
description: This was a waterthrush based on the short tail, white supercilium, pinkish legs, tail-bobbing, white breast with dark streaks.  The bird will be separated from Northern Waterthrush in the comparison portion of the summary.Behaviors: Fed constantly, mostly on small aquatic insects on rocks and at water's edge. Tail movement, especially a slow tail roll out to the left followed to a tail roll out to the right and then back to the left again, over and over.Call: none heardPlumage: alternate
